title: V5 P0 Generative UI, local collaboration, simulated execution, adapter, and runtime summary contract bundle
description: Machine-readable P0 contract for POST /api/generative-plan, local real collaboration state, simulated execution lifecycle, the independent frontend adapter, capability negotiation, feature flags, global references, GET /api/runtime summary draft, and preview-then-adopt candidate switching including standalone transport route-segment cards. P0 includes local persisted collaboration and simulated execution lifecycle; external real merchant, payment, message, and reservation platforms remain out of scope.

fallbackCopy.const: 已切换到稳定生成模式。

unsafeInputCopy.const: 需要确认一个安全信息后再继续。

conflictCopy.const: 当前修改会影响已锁定内容，已保留原方案。

included: share feedback persistence and return flow
audit events for share feedback and execution

x-versionCompatibilityContract.frontendDecision: Frontend recognizes version family instead of only exact version. If response is in v5 family and minimum UI Contract fields still validate, downgrade rendering is allowed.

knownTypePolicy.reason: Wire format allows forward-compatible type strings; renderer only executes known and whitelisted types.

renderingRules: If all requiredCapabilities are supported, frontend may render.
If optionalCapabilities are not supported, frontend must ignore or merge-degrade those parts.
Unknown card type must not enter the main render chain.
Unknown action type must hide the button and must not execute.
Unknown fields may be preserved in raw payload, meta, or extensions for debugging, but rendering and business logic must not depend on them.
Schema validation failure still triggers fallback.

x-p0LocalReplanContract.decision: Change one is a deterministic candidate switcher, not a random replacement. Preview never mutates Main; explicit adoption is the commit boundary.
x-p0LocalReplanContract.goal: Provide stable previous and next navigation, impact preview, explicit adoption, restoration, and one-step adoption undo.
requiredBehaviors: accurate previous navigation from local history
bounded next navigation with optional load more
preview and adopted states are visually distinct
preview does not mutate Main or saved snapshots
preview shows time budget timeline and risk changes
adoption revalidates time budget risk and schema
loading or validation failure preserves current candidate and Main
committed detail replan creates a new version and unsaved state
excludedBehaviors: more than five candidates per block
recommendedImplementationOrder: bounded load more with failure preservation
doneWhen: Previous restores the exact locally viewed candidate.
Next follows a stable ordered list and does not randomize.
Preview does not mutate Main or saved snapshots.
Adopt commits only after time budget risk and schema validation.
Restore original returns to the first generated content.
One undo is available after adoption.
Load or validation failure preserves the current candidate and Main.
Does not depend on backend true planning.
Does not affect old agent-core.js fallback.
Preview does not mutate collaboration, execution, audit, or SQLite state.

x-schemaAcceptanceContract.coreIdea: Use ui-contract.schema.json as the fact source, then generate types and validate backend responses, adapter outputs, and golden fixtures against it.

technicalTradeoff.reason: ui-contract.schema.json is a complete JSON Schema; hand-written validators are likely to miss fields and cost more over time.

acceptanceRules: ui-contract.schema.json is the source of truth for P0 UI Contract shape.
P0 must establish schema validation tests before implementation relies on generated types.
TypeScript types must be generated from ui-contract.schema.json or at least validated against it.
Python P0 validation uses JSON Schema first; a full Pydantic model hierarchy is not required in P0.
Backend contract tests must validate backend planned or mock responses against ui-contract.schema.json.
Adapter contract tests must validate agent-core adapter output against ui-contract.schema.json.
Fixture golden tests must validate shared golden fixtures against ui-contract.schema.json and stable snapshots.
Execution lifecycle contract tests must validate /api/executions create, query, advance, skip, cancel, and blocked paths.
Collaboration feedback contract tests must validate local share token, plan snapshot, feedback submit, read state, and owner return flow.
Local state persistence tests must validate SQLite share, feedback, execution, and audit write/readback.
Regeneration contract tests must validate /api/generative-plan regeneration with current snapshot, feedback summary, lineage, and adapter fallback.
Plan branch contract tests must validate one active main, up to 3 derived branches, proposed/adopted/rejected/archived statuses, adoption, rejection, previous-main rollback, and no-merge boundary.
All core responses must go through golden fixtures and contract tests.
Invalid fixtures must live under fixtures/v5/generative-plan/invalid/ and must not be used as frontend/backend shared render samples.

x-cardTypeWhitelist.scope: P0 card flow with local real collaboration and simulated execution lifecycle
x-cardTypeWhitelist.decision: Implement planning cards plus local share, feedback, and simulated execution summary cards in P0. External real execution and public collaboration remain out of scope.

p0MainRenderTypes.reason: Core card for validating that the V5 card flow works.

p0MainRenderTypes.purpose: Show default assumptions such as party size, budget, area, and time.
p0MainRenderTypes.reason: Handles ambiguous input without adding chatbot-style questioning cost.

p0MainRenderTypes.purpose: Show one actionable route segment bound to routeSegmentId, fromRef, and toRef.
p0MainRenderTypes.reason: Transport alternatives need independent preview, adoption, and downstream timeline impact.

p0MainRenderTypes.purpose: Ask for confirmation when safety or critical missing information exists.
p0MainRenderTypes.reason: Covers unsafe_input and avoids risky route gaps.

p0MainRenderTypes.purpose: Show local share token, snapshot, reviewer read state, and feedback count.
p0MainRenderTypes.reason: P0 includes local real collaboration state.

p0MainRenderTypes.purpose: Show collaborator feedback returned to the owner view.
p0MainRenderTypes.reason: P0 requires feedback persistence and return flow.

p0MainRenderTypes.purpose: Show simulated execution lifecycle status and step summary.

p0DeferredTypes.reason: P0 carries risk through plan_summary.riskText or soft_prompt. Dedicated risk cards move to P1.
Placeholder-only collaboration is superseded by share_summary and feedback_summary in the refrozen P0.

meaning.v5GenerativeUI: Default false so V5 cannot break the old demo path.
meaning.adapterFallback: Default true so unstable V5 can return to adapter fallback.
meaning.localReplan: Default true so local simulated replan remains allowed.
meaning.collaborationPlaceholder: Default false because P0 has local real collaboration state instead of placeholder-only collaboration.
meaning.executionImplementationRequired: Default false because V5 may consume a contract, fixture, adapter fallback, or degraded capability without requiring the V4 Runtime P0 implementation to be complete.
meaning.localCollaborationState: Default true inside V5 P0 because local share, feedback, read state, execution gate, and audit are P0 scope.
meaning.simulatedExecutionLifecycle: Default true inside V5 P0 because /api/executions lifecycle is P0 scope while external results remain mocked.

storagePolicy.purpose: local debugging and demo switching only

backendAuthority.rule: Backend can shrink capability but cannot force the frontend to enable V5 rendering when frontend effective v5GenerativeUI is false.

layerMeaning.contract_defaults: Safe contract defaults keep the old demo path working.
layerMeaning.client_effective_flags: Frontend computes whether to attempt V5 experiences from URL, storage, request, and safety guards.
layerMeaning.backend_capability_declaration: Backend declares current supported, degraded, or unsupported P0 capabilities and can only shrink client experience.
layerMeaning.safety_and_recovery_guards: Schema failure, unsafe input, timeout, version conflict, and recovery rules override flags and capabilities.

x-v5P0Contract.decision: V5 P0 implements local real collaboration state, simulated execution lifecycle, and a formal lightweight Plan Branch lifecycle. Local API, SQLite state, share page, feedback return flow, execution/step state, plan branch state, and audit are real; external execution results are mocked.

mockPart: external friend or family actually participating

sourcePolicy.reason: Distinguish backend mock/planned responses from legacy agent-core.js output converted by the adapter.

fixtureGenerationPolicy.conversionPath: agent-core.js plan -> agent-core-plan-to-ui-contract adapter -> V5 UI Contract JSON

validationRules: All shared fixtures must pass ui-contract.schema.json.
Success fixtures must be directly renderable by frontend card flow.
Adapter fallback fixture must prove legacy agent-core.js plan can become valid UI Contract.
All core responses must go through golden fixtures and contract tests.
POST /api/generative-plan P0 mock behavior must be fixture-driven; deterministic branching must not become a hidden planning engine.
Error fixtures must be structured envelopes, not natural-language dirty data examples.
Fixtures must use fixed UUID or ULID values to avoid snapshot churn.

topLevel.note: Convert the recommended plan id to Ref(kind=plan).
Each legacy plan generates a plan UIEntity and a plan_summary UICard.
Convert to simulated execution lifecycle actions and seed data only; do not call external platforms.
Use a short summary and selected evidence only; do not fully map trace internals.
Generate a soft_prompt card only when clarification is required.
Legacy parsed assumptions become editable assumption banner items when available.

plan.note: If the legacy id already uses plan-* keep it; otherwise normalize to plan-scoped id.
Generate only when risk content exists.
Score is metadata and must not be shown as the primary status.

restaurant.note: Do not invent wait information when missing.

timeline.note: P0 keeps the original label and does not force startTime/endTime parsing.
Use timeline-{planId}-{index} style scoped ids normalized to schema format.
Link to activity or restaurant when possible; otherwise use a placeholder entity, never point blindly to plan.

actions.note: Preview text is display-only and must not become executable UIAction.
P0 generates local simulated execution actions only.
Do not call a real backend reservation service.
P0 creates local share state and stores feedback locally; no external message platform is contacted.
Advance a persisted simulated execution step after /api/executions creates the record.
Only low-impact steps may be skipped in P0.
Cancel a single step and require reason, actor, time, affectedSteps, and audit.
Call /api/generative-plan with current snapshot and feedback summary; output becomes a derived branch.
Open a branch snapshot without mutating main.
Adopt a derived branch as the new active main and record previousMainBranchId.
Reject a derived branch while retaining audit history.
P0 rollback only returns to the immediately previous main.
Compatibility entry only. It initializes the candidate switcher or previews the next stable candidate and must not directly mutate Main.

assumptionBanner.note: When missing, show that the system temporarily uses default values; source should be local_rules or adapter_fallback.

riskNotice.note: Risks generate a separate card and must not be mixed into the normal plan summary; safety risks prefer soft_prompt.

degradation.note: Do not generate timeline cards when the source timeline is missing.
Do not render invalid V5 output.
